title: Realistic drivable car + realistic track scene matching the reference screenshot

requirements.text: Update the in-race player car visual model in the 3D race scene to look realistically like the uploaded reference screenshot: a modern wide-body sports coupe seen from the rear, primarily yellow bodywork, with a rainbow/gradient accent across the rear and a prominent rear spoiler, rendered with more realistic materials (paint + glass + rubber) than the current boxy placeholder car.

quotes: i want the car to look like this
1. yes. 2. yes, make it more realistic like that one. 3. the car should be drivable by the player

acceptanceCriteria: When launching the race scene (currently via Matchmaking -> Accept Match), the player's car is visually a realistic sports coupe (not the current simple box-based model).
The player car’s primary color reads as yellow, includes a visible rear rainbow/gradient accent, and has a clear rear spoiler element.
Car materials visually differ (e.g., glossy painted body, darker glass, rubber tires) under scene lighting.

requirements.text: Make the racing environment/track look more realistic and closer in look-and-feel to the uploaded screenshot: a road course with asphalt, grass verges, red/white curbs, guardrails/barriers, and trackside scenery, with lighting/fog tuned for realism.

quotes: 2. yes, make it more realistic like that one.

acceptanceCriteria: The track environment includes visible asphalt surface, grass off-track areas, and red/white curbing.
Trackside barriers/guardrails are present and visually consistent along the circuit.
Lighting and post effects (fog/sky) produce a more realistic outdoor track appearance than the current stylized arena-like ring.

requirements.text: Ensure the player can drive the car (player-controlled drivable gameplay) in the realistic scene with responsive controls, including acceleration/brake and steering, and with a stable third-person chase camera similar to the reference perspective (behind-the-car view).

quotes: 3. the car should be drivable by the player

acceptanceCriteria: Keyboard controls allow driving the car (accelerate/brake + steering) and the car’s position/rotation updates accordingly.
The camera follows behind the car in a chase view that keeps the car centered similarly to the reference screenshot while driving.
Driving remains playable without jitter (no extreme camera shaking or instability during normal driving).

requirements.text: Add a simple HUD overlay inspired by the reference screenshot that shows at minimum: current lap/total laps and current speed readout, in a clean, readable style that does not block the center of the screen.

quotes: yes, make it more realistic like that one.

acceptanceCriteria: During the race, UI displays lap progress (e.g., "Lap 1/3") and a speed value that changes as the car accelerates/brakes.
HUD elements are readable on top of the 3D scene and are placed away from the screen center.

requirements.text: Add any new required static art assets (textures/decals) as generated images under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference them directly from the frontend car/track rendering.

quotes: i want the car to look like this

acceptanceCriteria: All newly introduced textures/decals are stored in frontend/public/assets/generated and loaded directly by the frontend (e.g., via THREE.TextureLoader).
The realistic car appearance (yellow + rear gradient accent) is achieved using these static assets and/or materials in the scene.

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only UI component sources).
Do not edit frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, or frontend/src/main.tsx (immutable paths).
Keep all backend logic (if any is needed) within the single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; only add backend changes if required for the requested visuals/driving.
nonGoals: Building an exact 1:1 licensed recreation of the real-world track shown in the screenshot (no trademarked names/logos required).
Adding real-time multiplayer networking or authoritative physics simulation beyond the existing local/player control approach.
Adding third-party assets/services or external 3D model downloads.

required: Realistic sports coupe livery/texture: yellow body with a rear rainbow gradient band (sports-coupe-livery.dim_2048x1024.png)
Asphalt road texture with subtle wear and variation (asphalt-realistic.dim_2048x2048.png)
Grass ground texture suitable for trackside verges (grass-verge.dim_2048x2048.png)
